message:
fix(audit-chain): handle bounded retention in hash chain verification

Fix verify() to correctly validate chains after bounded retention has
evicted older entries. Previously, the first retained entry would fail
verification because its previous_hash pointed to an evicted predecessor
and was compared against an empty string.

Now seeds prev_hash from the first retained entry's recorded
previous_hash, treating it as an anchored head of the retained suffix.
This proves the retained window is self-consistent without falsely
flagging normal eviction as corruption.

- Update verify_chain_after_eviction test to assert retained suffix is valid
- Add verify_chain_after_eviction_still_detects_retained_link_corruption
  test to verify tampering within the retained window is still detected
- Expand doc comments explaining retained suffix verification semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
